title: Dose- and Ion-Dependent Effects in the Oxidative Stress Response to Space-Like Radiation Exposure in the Skeletal System
abstract: Space radiation may pose a risk to skeletal health during subsequent aging. Irradiation acutely stimulates bone remodeling in mice, although the long-term influence of space radiation on bone-forming potential and possible adaptive mechanisms are not well understood. This study shows that high-LET irradiation at 200 cGy impaired osteoblastogenesis and regulated steady-state gene expression of select redox-related genes, which may contribute to persistent bone loss.
source_pdf: Dose- and Ion-Dependent Effects in the Oxidative Stress Response to Space-Like Radiation Exposure in the Skeletal System.pdf

title: Cell Type-Specific Imaging of Calcium Signaling in Arabidopsis thaliana Seedling Roots Using GCaMP3
abstract: Cytoplasmic calcium is a well-characterized second messenger in eukaryotic cells. This paper introduces Arabidopsis lines expressing GCaMP3 in five root cell types, enabling the study of how environmental stimuli trigger calcium responses. Findings show distinct [Ca2+] signatures across cell types under ATP, glutamate, aluminum, and salt exposure.
source_pdf: Cell type-specific imaging of calcium signaling in Arabidopsis thaliana seedling roots using GCaMP3..pdf

title: Impact of microgravity and lunar gravity on murine skeletal and immune systems during space travel
abstract: Murine study aboard ISS comparing microgravity, lunar gravity (1/6 g) and 1 g; bone density and osteoblast/osteoclast markers show partial recovery at 1/6 g; thymus and spleen show gene expression changes.
source_pdf: Microgravity Stress Bone and Connective Tissue.pdf

title: Selective Proliferation of Highly Functional Adipose-Derived Stem Cells in Microgravity Culture with Stirred Microspheres
abstract: Simulated microgravity with stirred microspheres enriches SSEA‑3(+) hASCs, preserving stemness and enhancing proliferation/differentiation.
source_pdf: cells-10-00560.pdf

title: Muscle atrophy phenotype gene expression during spaceflight is linked to a metabolic crosstalk in both the liver and the muscle in mice
abstract: Transcriptomic comparison of liver and muscle during NASA spaceflight shows lipid/glucose pathway involvement; muscle atrophy associates with hepatic clusters, suggesting hepatokine‑mediated crosstalk.
source_pdf: Muscle atrophy phenotype gene expression during spaceflight is linked to a metabolic crosstalk in both the liver and the muscle in mice..pdf

title: Toward countering muscle and bone loss with spaceflight: GSK3 as a potential target
abstract: Following ~30 days of spaceflight, reduced GSK3β in muscle; muscle‑specific Gsk3 knockdown preserves strength and increases BMD in unloading models, nominating GSK3 as a countermeasure target.
source_pdf: Toward countering muscle and bone loss with spaceflight GSK3 as a potential target.pdf

title: Absence of γ-sarcoglycan alters the response of p70S6 kinase to mechanical perturbation in murine skeletal muscle
abstract: γ‑sarcoglycan deficiency decouples p70S6K activation in response to stretch, indicating altered mTOR‑dependent mechanotransduction in skeletal muscle.
source_pdf: Absence of gamma-sarcoglycan alters the response of p70S6 kinase to mechanical perturbation in murine skeletal muscle.pdf

title: Dried plum diet protects from bone loss caused by ionizing radiation
abstract: In mice, dried plum diet blunts pro‑resorptive gene expression after photon/HZE irradiation and prevents trabecular loss, suggesting a nutritional countermeasure for space radiation.
source_pdf: Dried plum diet protects from bone loss caused by ionizing radiation.pdf

title: Explainable machine learning identifies multi-omics signatures of muscle response to spaceflight in mice
abstract: Explainable ML (QLattice) integrates multi‑omics to highlight biomarkers (e.g., Acyp1, Rps7) linked to calcium reuptake and muscle resilience during spaceflight.
source_pdf: Explainable machine learning identifies multi-omics signatures of muscle response to spaceflight in mice.pdf

title: Single-cell RNA sequencing of the carotid and femoral artery of rats exposed to hindlimb unloading
abstract: scRNA‑seq in carotid and femoral arteries after 3 months of hindlimb unloading reveals increased endothelial proportion, reduced SMCs, fibroblast variability, and TF networks implicated in vascular remodeling.
source_pdf: ijms-26-03058.pdf

title: Microgravity and Cellular Biology: Insights into Cellular Responses and Implications for Human Health
abstract: Comprehensive review of cellular effects of microgravity across immunity, oncology, regeneration, hepatic metabolism, endothelium and bone; highlights Hippo and PI3K‑Akt pathways and terrestrial applications.
source_pdf: ijms-26-03058.pdf

title: Microgravity induces pelvic bone loss through osteoclastic activity, osteocytic osteolysis, and osteoblastic cell-cycle inhibition by CDKN1A/p21
abstract: STS‑131 mouse flight: decreased pelvic BV/TV and thickness; increased TRAP+ surface; nanoCT indicates lacunar osteolysis; MMP1/3/10 up; CDKN1A/p21 up and Trp53 down, suggesting resorption and osteogenic cell‑cycle arrest.
source_pdf: Microgravity induces pelvic bone loss through osteoclastic activity, osteocytic osteolysis, and osteoblastic cell cycle inhibition by CDKN1ap21.pdf

title: Osteocyte apoptosis caused by hindlimb unloading is required to trigger osteocyte RANKL production and subsequent resorption of cortical and trabecular bone in mice femurs
abstract: In HLU mice, osteocyte apoptosis rises (day 5) preceding RANKL upregulation and bone resorption in cortical and trabecular compartments; pan‑caspase inhibitor QVD blocks apoptosis, RANKL, resorption, and prevents trabecular loss.
venue: Journal of Bone and Mineral Research
topic: HLU bone loss via osteocyte apoptosis
source_pdf: Osteocyte apoptosis caused by hindlimb unloading is required to trigger osteocyte RANKL production and subsequent resorption of cortical and trabecular bone in mice femurs..pdf

title: Partial reductions in mechanical loading yield proportional changes in bone density, bone architecture, and muscle mass
abstract: Using a partial weight suspension system in mice (20–100% weight bearing) for 21 days, bone and muscle changes scale linearly with unloading; even 70% WB causes significant deterioration; HLU produces the largest effects.
venue: Journal of Bone and Mineral Research
source_pdf: Partial reductions in mechanical loading yield proportional changes in bone density, bone architecture, and muscle mass.pdf

title: Piezo1 regulates the regenerative capacity of skeletal muscles via orchestration of stem cell morphological states
abstract: In vivo imaging reveals quiescent MuSC heterogeneity; Piezo1 controls transitions between sensory/intermediate/responsive states; pharmacologic activation primes responsive cells, while deletion impairs regeneration and mimics dystrophy.
source_pdf: Piezo1 regulates the regenerative capacity of skeletal muscles via orchestration of stem cell morphological states..pdf

title: Mice in Bion-M 1 Space Mission: Training and Selection
abstract: Overview of Bion‑M1 30‑day mission; describes mouse housing/training, group co‑adaptation and paste diet; post‑flight mice suitable for biomedical studies but show pronounced readaptation challenges.
source_pdf: pone.0104830.pdf

title: RNAseq and RNA molecular barcoding reveal differential gene expression in cortical bone following hindlimb unloading in female mice
abstract: Whole‑transcriptome profiling of cortical bone after 7‑day HLU identifies changes in Wnt/metabolism and novel mechanosensitive genes (e.g., Pfkfb3, Mss51), advancing understanding of disuse osteoporosis.
source_pdf: RNAseq and RNA molecular barcoding reveal differential gene expression in cortical bone following hindlimb unloading in female mice.pdf

title: Spaceflight and hind limb unloading induce similar changes in electrical impedance characteristics of mouse gastrocnemius muscle
abstract: EIM on ex vivo gastrocnemius shows reduced multifrequency phase‑slope after spaceflight (STS‑135) or 13‑day HLU versus controls; parameter correlates with fiber size and hind limb aBMD.
source_pdf: Spaceflight and hind limb unloading induce similar changes in electrical impedance characteristics of mouse gastrocnemius muscle.pdf

title: Spaceflight-Induced Bone Loss Alters Failure Mode and Reduces Bending Strength in Murine Spinal Segments
abstract: Mice from 30‑day Bion M1 mission exhibit shorter toe region and 17% lower bending strength; failures shift to growth plate/epiphyseal bone; μCT shows reduced BV/TV, BMD and trabecular thickness.
source_pdf: Spaceflight-induced bone loss alters failure mode and reduces bending strength in murine spinal segments..pdf

title: Treatment with a soluble bone morphogenetic protein type 1A receptor (BMPR1A) fusion protein increases bone mass and bone formation in mice subjected to hindlimb unloading
abstract: In 21‑day HLU, mBMPR1A‑mFc preserves leg BMD and enhances trabecular/cortical microarchitecture and failure load; histomorphometry shows increased formation and reduced resorption, supporting BMP pathway modulation as countermeasure.
source_pdf: Treatment with a soluble bone morphogenetic protein type 1A receptor (BMPR1A) fusion protein increases bone mass and bone formation in mice subjected to hindlimb unloading..pdf
